rulings.Case Title: SATISH GUPTA VS. DANIEL LIPSITT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 2: Plaintiff Gupta Satish's Motion For Summary Judgment. GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burden and opposing party admits that the only issue is an issue of law, i.e. interpretation of SFRO 37.2(r)(5). The Court finds based on Da Vinci v. San Francisco Rent Board (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 24 that the date of issuance of the Certificate of Occupancy is not determinative in regards to the applicability of the exemption; the use of the premises prior to the effective date of the rent ordinance and the date of conversion to residential use are determinative. Facts are undisputed here that the property has not been converted to residential use until after 1980, i.e. building was restructured to permit new residential use after 1979. Additionally, Certificate of Occupancy issued on 6/17/14 relates back and is expressly based on the inspection conducted in 2003. =(501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: SATISH GUPTA VS. DANIEL LIPSITT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Failure To Oppose Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Judgment And/Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT DANIEL LIPSITT'S Motion For Relief From Failure To Oppose Plaintiff's Motion For Summary Judgment And/Or Adjudication. GRANTED. Judgment and August 6, 2014 Order Granting Motion for Summary is hereby vacated. Defendant must remit $2,587.44 to Plaintiff's counsel no later than September 2, 2014 for attorney's fees and cost incurred in obtaining the judgment. =(501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE VS. TIFFANY COFFMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 29, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT TIFFANY COFFMAN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. GRANTED subject to: (1) opposition; (2) Defendant posting $10,030 with the Court (rent demanded in the notice, plus August and September daily damages) no later than September 2, 2014 by 11:00 a.m. or providing evidence that rent demanded in the notice, plus daily damages for August and September have been paid by the Education Opportunity Program and/or CalWorks no later than September 2, 2014 by 11:00 a.m. at the ex-parte hearing in Department 501; and (3) Defendant filing an answer no later than September 2, 2014. Answer shall not be filed unless the posting is made or evidence of payment is presented. =(501/LKS)
